display_name: Fish ingestion rate - ACFN community-specific subsistence (WQCIU 2023, Lower Athabasca)
applicability: ACFN/FMFN/MCFN community-specific adult fish ingestion rate (WQCIU 2023, Olsgard et al.; Chapter 3, Section 3.4.5; adopted in Equation 3.2 at document p.154; Figure 3.2 and discussion at document p.206). 388.1 g/day = 0.388 kg/day is the HIGHEST community (Community 1) 95th-percentile heavy-consumer rate, adopted by WQCIU as protective for traditional-food consumers; fish is used as a surrogate for traditional foods. display_name: Skin surface area, total body - construction/utility worker (HC PQRA v4.0 Appendix E)
applicability: Health Canada PQRA v4.0 (2024) Appendix E recommended TOTAL-BODY skin surface area for the construction/utility worker: 16640 cm2 (owner-attested correction, see below). The PQRA v4.0 Appendix E table prints '1 640' cm2 for this cell; that figure is an apparent Health Canada typesetting error for 16,640 cm2 (a dropped leading '16') and is impossible as printed: the construction/utility worker shares the adult 70.7 kg body, whose own Appendix E regional rows (hands ~890 + arms ~2500 + legs ~5720 -- owner/domain reasoning summing the regional cells, NOT a separate catalog record) already total ~9110 cm2, so a total body of 1,640 would be smaller than three of its own regions; 16,640 sits just below the adult total-body 17,640, the expected magnitude. Corrected to 16640 cm2 on owner attestation 2026-06-13. Underlying source Richardson (1997), tabulated in Appendix E. Substance-independent receptor characteristic (substance_key=generic). needs_review candidate; not a calculator default. [PROMOTED to approved (evidence approved_source_backed, source direct_source_verified) on 2026-06-13 by J. Nelson; the pending / needs_review language above is superseded.]
uncertainty: Appendix E also tabulates surface area split by body region (hands, arms, legs, feet); this row is the total-body sum. For dermal dose, the total-body SA pairs with the "surfaces other than hands" soil-loading value (hands are ~5% of total-body area and carry a higher loading, a second-order refinement). Measure of central tendency: arithmetic mean of log-normal pdfs (Richardson 1997). DATA-INTEGRITY NOTE (2026-06-13, owner-attested): the PQRA v4.0 Appendix E cell prints '1 640' cm2 for the worker total body; this value is corrected to 16640 cm2 as an apparent HC typesetting error (a dropped leading '16'), being internally impossible as printed (smaller than its own hands+arms+legs regional sum) while 16,640 is the expected magnitude for the 70.7 kg adult-bodied worker receptor. This SUPERSEDES the prior interpretation (PR #302) that framed 1,640 as an intentional PPE / limited-exposed-skin value; that reading is not grounded in Appendix E, which has no exposed-skin row and pairs anatomical total-body / regional areas with separate per-region soil-loading factors. No independent HC erratum or accessible Richardson (1997) primary was located; the correction rests on physical / internal-consistency inference plus owner attestation. [PROMOTED to approved (evidence approved_source_backed, source direct_source_verified) on 2026-06-13 by J. Nelson; the pending / needs_review language above is superseded.]

display_name: Fish ingestion rate - toddler subsistence (TWN Burrard Inlet WQO 2021)
applicability: TWN Burrard Inlet WQO toddler subsistence fish ingestion rate (ENV and HLTH 2021, Table 1; 94 g/day = 0.094 kg/day; aspirational Indigenous-subsistence basis, Richardson 1997). Tsleil-Waututh Nation (TWN) + Province of B.C. co-governance source. CAVEAT (report Sec 2.1.1): the TWN BIWQO tissue screening VALUES are derived for ambient water quality objectives and the report states they should NOT be used to derive remediation or BC Contaminated Sites Regulation guidelines; this catalog cites only the receptor consumption-rate EXPOSURE FACTOR (94 g/day), not the report's tissue screening values. Substance-independent exposure factor (substance_key=generic). Toddler subsistence receptor (DIFFERENT from the adult subsistence rate of 220 g/day). needs_review candidate; not a calculator default pending owner promotion. [PROMOTED to approved (evidence approved_source_backed, source direct_source_verified) on 2026-06-15 by J. Nelson; the pending / needs_review language above is superseded.]

display_name: Toddler body weight - subsistence food-web receptor (HC PQRA v4.0 Appendix E, food-web pathway)
applicability: Standard Canadian toddler body weight (16.5 kg) per Health Canada PQRA v4.0 (2024) Appendix E (Table: Recommended Receptor Characteristics for HHRAs; underlying source Richardson 1997). This is the STANDARD HC toddler body weight adopted as the food-web pathway BW for the TWN Burrard Inlet WQO toddler subsistence receptor. It is PAIRED WITH the TWN BIWQO 2021 toddler subsistence fish-ingestion rate (pv-twn-biwqo-2021-ir-food-toddler-bc; 0.094 kg/day, 94 g/day from ENV and HLTH 2021 Table 1): the TWN source defines the community-specific consumption rate; the toddler body weight is the standard HC PQRA v4.0 value (same value as pv-hc-pqra-v4-2024-bw-toddler-ca under the human-health-direct pathway). This is a MIXED-SOURCE receptor: IR_food=TWN BIWQO 2021, BW=HC PQRA v4.0. Substance-independent exposure factor (substance_key=generic). NOTE on provenance: the TWN BIWQO 2021 report does NOT tabulate a toddler body weight; it tabulates only an ADULT body weight (76.5 kg) as a distinct TWN-sourced value. The 16.5 kg toddler body weight here is the standard HC PQRA v4.0 value (not a TWN figure), which is why this record is sourced to HC PQRA v4.0. needs_review candidate; not a calculator default pending owner promotion. [PROMOTED to approved (evidence approved_source_backed, source direct_source_verified) on 2026-06-15 by J. Nelson; the pending / needs_review language above is superseded.]
uncertainty: The 16.5 kg toddler body weight is the Health Canada PQRA v4.0 (2024) Appendix E standard value (Richardson 1997 basis). This food-web record reuses the same HC value under the human-health-food pathway for pairing with the TWN BIWQO 2021 toddler subsistence IR_food. Secondary citation pending direct-source verification against the HC PQRA v4.0 primary PDF before any HITL promotion. [PROMOTED to approved (evidence approved_source_backed, source direct_source_verified) on 2026-06-15 by J. Nelson; the pending / needs_review language above is superseded.]
